title: How to create a GitHub repository with README and license

steps.title: Navigate to GitHub and access repository creation
steps.description: Go to GitHub.com and sign in to your account. Once you're logged in, you'll see your dashboard where you can start creating your new repository.
steps.action: Navigate to github.com, click Sign In, enter credentials, then locate the green 'New' button or '+' icon in the top-right corner

steps.title: Start creating a new repository
steps.description: Click on the '+' icon in the top-right corner of any GitHub page. From the dropdown menu, select 'New repository' to open the repository creation form.
steps.action: Click the '+' icon in top-right corner, then click 'New repository' from dropdown menu

steps.title: Configure repository name and description
steps.description: Enter a unique repository name (up to 100 characters) that describes your project. Optionally, add a brief description to help others understand what your project does.
steps.action: Type repository name in the 'Repository name' field, then type optional description in the 'Description' field

steps.title: Set repository visibility
steps.description: Choose whether your repository will be public (visible to everyone) or private (only visible to you and collaborators). For open source projects, select public visibility.
steps.action: Click the 'Public' radio button for open source projects, or 'Private' for personal/restricted projects

steps.title: Initialize repository with README file
steps.description: Check the 'Add a README file' checkbox to create a default README.md file. This file will be displayed on your repository's main page and should contain information about your project.
steps.action: Check the checkbox next to 'Add a README file' to enable it

steps.title: Select a license for your project
steps.description: Click on the 'Choose a license' dropdown menu to select an appropriate license for your project. Popular options include MIT License for permissive open source projects or Apache 2.0 for more comprehensive licensing.
steps.action: Click 'Choose a license' dropdown, browse available licenses, then select appropriate license (e.g., MIT License, Apache 2.0)

steps.title: Create the repository
steps.description: Review all your settings to ensure everything is configured correctly. Once satisfied, click the green 'Create repository' button to finalize the creation of your new GitHub repository with README and license files.
steps.action: Review all settings, then click the green 'Create repository' button at the bottom of the form
